ToList() time complexity is?

The `ToList()` method in C# converts an `IEnumerable` collection into a `List`. The time complexity of the `ToList()` method is O(n), where n is the number of elements in the source `IEnumerable` collection.

When you call the `ToList()` method on an `IEnumerable` collection, it iterates over each element in the collection and adds it to a new `List`. Therefore, the time complexity is O(n), where n is the number of elements in the source collection.

It's important to keep this time complexity in mind when using the `ToList()` method, especially if you are working with large collections, as it involves iterating over all the elements to create a new list.
